prompt: Write Bruno tests for a REST API that manages blog posts (CRUD). Base URL is http://localhost:3000. Auth is via Bearer token from env var API_TOKEN. Endpoints: POST /posts (create), GET /posts/:id (read), PUT /posts/:id (update), DELETE /posts/:id (delete). Posts have id, title, body, authorId, createdAt, updatedAt. Title is required and max 200 chars. Body is required. authorId is a UUID. Cover happy paths, validation errors, and 404s.

assertions.name: has-opencollection-yml
assertions.description: opencollection.yml exists with name, version, type fields

assertions.name: has-environment-file
assertions.description: environments/local.yml exists bridging process.env.BASE_URL and process.env.API_TOKEN

assertions.name: folder-level-auth
assertions.description: At least one folder.yml contains Bearer token auth in before-request script

assertions.name: happy-path-coverage
assertions.description: At least one happy path test per CRUD operation (create, read, update, delete)

assertions.name: error-case-coverage
assertions.description: Tests exist for missing required fields (title, body) or invalid authorId

assertions.name: 404-coverage
assertions.description: At least one test for 404 on nonexistent resource

assertions.name: uses-opencollection-yaml
assertions.description: Test files use OpenCollection YAML format (info/http/runtime keys), not Bru markup

assertions.name: state-sharing
assertions.description: Uses bru.setVar to store created post ID for use in subsequent tests

assertions.name: correct-assertions
assertions.description: Tests use Chai expect() with status code and body property assertions
